scope.objective: Harden Passport during development by keeping Canton ledger semantics, framework-neutral interop, generated artifacts, and release boundaries tied to explicit invariants and executable gates.
assumptions: Daml templates remain the normative Canton ledger schema for this milestone.
FINOS CDM support means offline JSON-schema conformance for generated collateral eligibility artifacts, not FINOS certification, Rosetta Engine execution, or CDM eligibility-engine execution.
Default PR CI must not fetch network resources after GitHub platform checkout; dependency and DPM toolchains are provided by a pre-baked offline runner.
The CDM schema manifest provides committed local integrity for reviewed schema files, not upstream authenticity when schema and manifest are changed together.
Adapter readiness claims are evidence-bound: Level 2 means offline artifact conformance only until executable, sandbox, or production evidence is committed.
Registered adapters cannot use Level 0; Level 0 is concept-only for future adapter candidates with no registered plugin implementation.
Level 3 and higher readiness cannot be self-attested by category names alone or generic existing files; higher levels require existing references in category-appropriate proof-bearing paths.

frontier.selection_reason: Round 0004 tightens Adapter Readiness Level enforcement with Level 0 registration rejection, category-specific proof-reference validation, sentence-level overclaim fixtures, report consistency checks, and narrower CDM payload metadata bans.
kill_gates: hardening:gate fails if a registered plugin uses concept-only Level 0 readiness.

hardening:gate fails if Level 3+ readiness is self-attested by category names or generic existing files such as package.json, README, or generic docs without category-appropriate proof-bearing references.
hardening:gate fails if docs or guidance contain forbidden Level 2 overclaim phrases without same-sentence negation or promotion-boundary language, and negative fixtures prove rejected examples fail.
hardening:gate fails if artifacts/interop/report.json readiness drifts from registered plugin readiness summaries or includes unregistered adapters.
hardening:gate fails if generated CDM payloads contain Passport provenance or readiness metadata while avoiding broad bans on future non-CDM domain keys.

invariants.statement: Only the intended parties can create, present, reserve, revoke, or disclose credential state.

properties.statement: Every externally relevant choice is controlled by the holder, attester, verifier, or publisher role that owns the transition.
Credential and audit data is visible only to signatories, intended observers, or scoped auditors.

invariants.statement: Reservation transitions cannot create more reservable capacity than the presented credential permits.

properties.statement: A reservation must be positive and cannot exceed the presented reservable amount.
Residual credentials are minted only for presented reservable amount minus reserved amount.

invariants.statement: The foundation package models collateral capacity credentials only, without expanding into custody, settlement, legal title, credit approval, or proof systems.

properties.statement: New domain types must stay within the collateral credential account boundary unless an ADR updates the scope.

invariants.statement: External templates and domain choices stay covered by Daml Script tests.

properties.statement: The Daml test command must fail if external template or domain-choice coverage drops below 100%.

invariants.statement: Every framework adapter exposes the same minimal generate and validate contract without arbitrary execution privileges.

properties.statement: Plugins must define id, framework, frameworkVersion, outputFormat, artifactTypes, generate, and validate before registration.
The adapter context must state that dynamic plugins, eval, and network access in CI are disabled.

invariants.statement: Adapter maturity claims are capped by machine-readable readiness metadata, committed evidence references, generated reports, sentence-bounded docs, and hardening gates.

properties.statement: An adapter may not be registered, documented, reported, or marketed above the highest readiness level supported by committed category-appropriate tests, reports, proof-bearing paths, and gates; registered plugins cannot use concept-only Level 0.

A Level 2 artifact-conformance adapter must not be described in same-sentence claim units as live integration, external engine execution, certification, custody, settlement, production partner integration, or Canton Token Standard integration unless the sentence explicitly negates the claim or states the higher-level promotion boundary.

Interop reports must publish adapter readiness level, evidence, claims, non-claims, and promotion criteria for every registered adapter, and report readiness must deeply match registered plugin readiness summaries.

invariants.statement: Repo-authored framework adapters are registered statically and cannot be loaded from remote code, arbitrary paths, runtime strings, or dynamic execution primitives.

properties.statement: The repo-authored adapter registry imports known plugins statically, exports a frozen registry, and does not use dynamic plugin execution.

invariants.statement: Framework payloads remain clean while Passport provenance and adapter readiness metadata are recorded in adapter metadata and reports.

properties.statement: Generated framework payload files must not contain Passport provenance or adapter readiness metadata keys; generic words are banned only where they would be leaked CDM readiness report metadata.

invariants.statement: The CDM plugin generates Passport collateral eligibility artifacts and validates their JSON shape against pinned FINOS CDM 6.0 schemas without executing a CDM eligibility engine.

properties.statement: EligibleCollateralSpecification, EligibilityQuery, and CheckEligibilityResult are generated from one Passport repo pre-trade sample.
The validator must prove at least one malformed CDM payload fails schema validation.
CheckEligibilityResult reports must warn that the Passport sample eligibility decision is mirrored and no CDM eligibility engine is executed.

invariants.statement: CDM schema validation is offline and reproducible from committed plugin-scoped schema files whose local manifest hashes and schema-set digest are verified before validation.

properties.statement: Committed schema hashes and the schema-set digest must be verified before AJV registers schemas for validation; this is local integrity, not upstream authenticity.
Repo-authored network fetches are allowed only in the explicit CDM vendor refresh path, never in default validation or CI.

invariants.statement: Skills used for this repo are checked into the repository in Codex's repo skill discovery path and are referenced by project instructions.

properties.statement: The Passport hardening skill must live under `.agents/skills` so other Codex agents launched in the repo can discover it.
Root AGENTS.md must instruct future agents when to use the Passport hardening loop.

functions.name: checkAdapterReadiness, readiness negative cases, sentence-level prose checks, and interop report consistency

invariants.statement: The default PR and local CI paths run from a pre-baked offline toolchain, cover generated artifacts, hardening controls, repository gates, packaging, Daml tests, Canton smoke, and freshness checks.

properties.statement: npm run ci must execute npm run hardening:gate before Daml and Canton smoke tests.
Default PR CI must not run networked bootstrap, dependency install, schema refresh, or arbitrary network primitives after GitHub platform checkout.
Default CI must fail when regenerated tracked artifacts, invariant maps, or frontiers differ from committed output.
Every hardening-sensitive tracked workflow, package, Daml, interop, script, policy, map, frontier, round, and change-log file must appear in the invariant map source inventory.
npm run ci must run npm run package so release archive regressions cannot bypass the default green bar.
The AJV transitive require-from-string dependency is allowed only as the pinned package-lock edge from AJV and is not a repo-authored dynamic execution mechanism.

invariants.statement: The generated release archive includes reviewable source, docs, hardening artifacts, and generated interop artifacts while excluding build/cache output.

properties.statement: Packaging must use tracked files and exclude Daml build coverage output.

breakdowns.description: A hardening skill is stored outside Codex's repo skill discovery path, so future agents do not see it automatically.
A party outside the intended holder, attester, verifier, or publisher set attempts to exercise a state-changing choice.
breakdowns.kill_gate: Daml Script negative tests and coverage gate fail when external authorization coverage drops.

breakdowns.description: A verifier tries to reserve more than the presented reservable amount.
breakdowns.kill_gate: Reservation negative tests must reject over-reservation.

breakdowns.description: A future adapter change loads framework plugins from runtime strings, remote code, or arbitrary paths.
breakdowns.kill_gate: hardening:gate rejects dynamic imports, eval-style execution, and non-static registry shape.

breakdowns.description: A committed schema file changes without matching local manifest hashes or schema-set digest, weakening offline validation.
breakdowns.kill_gate: CDM validation verifies committed plugin-scoped manifest hashes and schema-set digest before AJV registration.

breakdowns.description: Passport metadata is embedded inside framework payloads instead of adapter reports.
breakdowns.kill_gate: hardening:gate scans generated CDM payload keys for Passport provenance fields.

breakdowns.description: A default PR or local CI command fetches schemas, dependencies, toolchains, plugin code, or other mutable network resources.
breakdowns.kill_gate: hardening:gate scans workflow YAML, shell scripts, package scripts, and repo-authored JS for network primitives, allowing only the explicit CDM vendor refresh module and offline npm ci.

breakdowns.description: Generated reports, maps, or frontiers are refreshed during CI but stale committed copies still pass.
breakdowns.kill_gate: npm run ci finishes with git diff --exit-code over tracked artifacts, hardening maps, and hardening frontiers.

hardening:gate rejects Level 0 registered plugins, missing readiness metadata, unknown levels, level/name mismatches, evidence-category gaps, missing proof-bearing evidence references, generic existing-file proof references for Level 3+, and unknown lastVerifiedBy commands.

hardening:gate rejects Level 2 overclaim sentences unless the same claim unit explicitly negates the claim or states the higher-level promotion boundary; negative prose fixtures prove bad claims fail.

interop:validate and hardening:gate fail when registered adapters are absent from report readiness, unregistered adapters appear, or readiness fields drift between report.adapters, report.adapterReadiness, and registered plugin summaries.

exploit_paths.impact: Future agents make hardening-sensitive changes without using the invariant map, frontier, and change-log workflow.
preconditions: Repo-local skills are not stored under `.agents/skills` or root AGENTS.md does not point agents to them.

exploit_paths.impact: Unauthorized reservation or disclosure of credential-derived capacity state.
preconditions: A non-controller party can exercise a ledger choice.

exploit_paths.impact: Reservable collateral capacity can be inflated beyond the attested amount.
preconditions: ReserveFromPresentation accepts reservedAmount greater than presentedReservableAmount.

exploit_paths.impact: Interop validation executes unreviewed framework adapter code.
preconditions: The registry accepts runtime plugin paths or code strings.

exploit_paths.impact: Generated artifacts appear conformant against altered or incomplete schemas.
preconditions: Schema files can be changed without manifest hash verification.

exploit_paths.impact: Framework artifacts stop being pure CDM objects and become Passport-specific envelopes.
preconditions: Generated payload files include Passport metadata keys.

exploit_paths.impact: Default CI depends on mutable remote data.
preconditions: A CI command fetches network content outside explicit vendoring.

exploit_paths.impact: Reviewers merge stale generated evidence that no longer matches source-controlled generators or hardening policy.
preconditions: A generator rewrites tracked artifacts and CI does not diff the regenerated output.

trust_boundaries.name: Repo-local agent skill discovery
trust_boundaries.description: Repository instructions and skills must be discoverable by agents launched inside the repo.

trust_boundaries.name: Ledger party authority
trust_boundaries.description: Daml controllers and signatories decide who can mutate ledger state.

trust_boundaries.name: Interop plugin boundary
trust_boundaries.description: Framework adapters convert Passport samples into external framework artifacts without runtime plugin loading.

trust_boundaries.name: Explicit schema vendor refresh
trust_boundaries.description: Network access is isolated to an explicit schema refresh command and pinned by a manifest.

trust_boundaries.name: Review archive boundary
trust_boundaries.description: The default CI and review package should contain deterministic source and review artifacts, not stale generated output, build caches, or local logs.
